title: juncaceae
intro: The Juncaceae rank refers to a family of flowering plants, commonly known as the rush family. This family consists of approximately 430 species, including rushes, sedges, and club rushes, which are typically found in wetland environments. Juncaceae plants are typically herbaceous, with slender stems and narrow, pointed leaves. They can be found in a wide range of habitats, including marshes, meadows, and even urban areas, and are often used in landscaping for their ornamental and erosion-control properties.
